series.title: Envoy AI Gateway v1.0.x
series.subtitle: The first stable, generally available release. The v1beta1 control-plane API is now covered by our stability guarantee — with 16 LLM providers, an MCP gateway, multimodal and audio endpoints, enterprise observability, and multi-tenant routing, all on the proven Envoy Gateway foundation.

releases.overview: Envoy AI Gateway v1.0.0 marks General Availability. With this release the core control-plane API — AIGatewayRoute, AIServiceBackend, BackendSecurityPolicy, GatewayConfig, and MCPRoute, all served at v1beta1 — is declared stable: within the 1.x series we will not make breaking changes to it unless required by a critical security fix, and any such change will ship with a documented migration path. Upgrading from v0.7 requires no changes to your resources. 1.0 brings together everything built since the first release in February 2025: a single OpenAI-compatible API across 16 providers with cross-provider translation, a full Model Context Protocol gateway, multimodal and audio endpoints, enterprise-grade observability, and multi-tenant, quota-aware routing — all as an additive layer on CNCF Envoy Gateway. This is the foundation we set out to build when we said “Onward to 1.0”: a stable base you can run production AI infrastructure on.

items.title: Core CRDs now covered by the stability guarantee
items.description: The control-plane API you build on — AIGatewayRoute, AIServiceBackend, BackendSecurityPolicy, GatewayConfig, and MCPRoute, all served at v1beta1 — is now a stable contract. Within the 1.x series these APIs will not change in a breaking way unless required by a critical security fix, and any such change will ship with a documented migration path. You can standardize on Envoy AI Gateway without tracking a moving target.

items.title: One OpenAI-compatible API across 16 providers
items.description: Reach OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Google Gemini, Google Vertex AI, AWS Bedrock, Anthropic, Mistral, Cohere, Groq, Together AI, DeepInfra, DeepSeek, Hunyuan, SambaNova, Grok, and the Tetrate Agent Router Service through a single endpoint. Point your application at the gateway once and switch or mix providers without changing client code.

items.title: Cross-provider request/response translation
items.description: Translate between provider protocols transparently — Anthropic /v1/messages to OpenAI /v1/chat/completions, and Anthropic Messages to AWS Bedrock Converse and InvokeModel — including streaming, tool use, reasoning/thinking blocks, and images, so clients reach any backend without rewriting their integration.

items.title: Model virtualization with modelNameOverride
items.description: Expose stable, application-facing model names while the gateway maps them to provider-specific models. Enables A/B testing, gradual migrations, and multi-provider strategies without touching client code, and guards against provider lock-in.

items.title: Chat, completions, embeddings, and images
items.description: /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions, /v1/embeddings, and /v1/images/generations are supported across compatible providers, all behind the same auth, rate limiting, and observability.

items.title: Audio: transcription, translation, and speech
items.description: /v1/audio/transcriptions, /v1/audio/translations, and /v1/audio/speech bring speech-to-text and text-to-speech workloads through the gateway alongside the rest of your AI traffic.

items.title: OpenAI Responses API and multimodal inputs
items.description: The /v1/responses endpoint is supported, including on Azure OpenAI backends, and chat requests accept image, audio_url, and video_url content parts for compatible backends.

items.title: Aggregate and route Model Context Protocol servers
items.description: Multiplex multiple MCP servers behind one endpoint with MCPRoute, including tool routing and include/exclude filtering, so clients see one unified tool surface.

items.title: Fine-grained, CEL-based authorization
items.description: Enforce per-tool authorization. tools/list applies the same rules as tools/call, so callers only discover the tools they are allowed to invoke — no leaked tool names, no wasted LLM turns.

items.title: Per-backend header forwarding with JWT claim projection
items.description: Forward selected request headers and project JWT claims to individual MCP backends, enabling identity-aware access to downstream tool servers.

items.title: Hostname-based multi-tenant routing
items.description: Serve different model sets per hostname from a single Gateway with AIGatewayRoute.spec.hostnames; the /v1/models endpoint scopes its response to the models for the matching host.

items.title: Token- and quota-aware rate limiting
items.description: Rate limit on model tokens and per QuotaPolicy, with backend rate limit filter injection to enforce quota-based throttling against upstream provider limits.

items.title: Provider fallback and InferencePool support
items.description: Automatic failover across providers, plus intelligent endpoint selection for self-hosted models via the Gateway API Inference Extension.

items.title: BackendSecurityPolicy for upstream authentication
items.description: Centralize provider credentials with API key, AWS, Azure, and GCP cloud-native identity — including GKE Workload Identity via Application Default Credentials — keeping secrets out of application code.

items.title: Request/response body redaction
items.description: Redact sensitive request and response bodies to meet compliance requirements without losing the rest of your observability.

items.title: OpenTelemetry tracing with OpenInference
items.description: Full request-lifecycle tracing, compatible with AI evaluation tools like Arize Phoenix, for deep visibility into every LLM and MCP request.

items.title: GenAI token metrics and reasoning-token accounting
items.description: Prometheus metrics for token usage, time-to-first-token, and inter-token latency, with separate accounting for reasoning tokens so cost attribution stays accurate for thinking models.

apiChanges.title: The v1beta1 API is now stable
apiChanges.description: v1.0 does not change the API surface. Instead it elevates the existing v1beta1 CRDs to a stable contract under our support policy: no new apiVersion is introduced and no resource migration is required. New fields added during the 1.x series will remain backward compatible.

dependencies.title: Envoy Gateway v1.8.1
dependencies.description: Built on Envoy Gateway v1.8.1 for proven data plane capabilities.

dependencies.title: Envoy v1.38
dependencies.description: Leveraging Envoy Proxy v1.38.1 for battle-tested networking.

dependencies.title: Gateway API v1.5.1
dependencies.description: Support for Gateway API v1.5.1 specifications.

dependencies.title: Gateway API Inference Extension v1.0.2
dependencies.description: Continued integration with Gateway API Inference Extension v1.0.2 for intelligent endpoint selection.

dependencies.title: MCP Go SDK v1.6.1
dependencies.description: Built on MCP Go SDK v1.6.1 for the latest Model Context Protocol features.
